subject: Computer Networking
title: POP3 — Downloading Mail
description: A micro-lesson that makes POP3 — Downloading Mail usable.

hook.text: Before IMAP, there was POP3. Download your mail, delete it from the server. Simple and offline.

buildup.text: POP3 connects to the server, downloads all new messages, and optionally deletes the server copies.

discovery.text: Once downloaded, emails live only on your device. No sync, no multi-device access by default.

twist.text: POP3 is still useful: low-bandwidth areas, archival setups, or privacy-focused users who want local-only storage.

climax.text: Some clients keep copies on the server with 'leave on server.' A workaround, but not true sync.

punchline.text: POP3 downloads and deletes. Simple, but not synced.

quiz.question: What happens by default after POP3 downloads email?
options: The email is marked as read on all devices
A backup is sent to a second server
